Body’s instant signals.<[silence:600ms]> Decisions before thinking.<[silence:600ms]>

The word emotion comes from Latin emovere—“to move out.” Like a storm shifting sand, emotions push thoughts and actions before logic catches up.<[silence:600ms]>

A smile flashes in 0.3 seconds when joy hits, faster than blinking.<[silence:600ms]> Fear triggers adrenaline in 1.5 seconds, sharpening focus like a camera zoom.<[silence:600ms]> These aren’t just feelings;<[silence:200ms]> they’re survival tools.<[silence:600ms]> Anger tightens muscles for conflict.<[silence:600ms]> Disgust wrinkles the nose to block bad smells.<[silence:600ms]> Even “useless” emotions serve:<[silence:200ms]> embarrassment’s blush cools overheated skin after stress.<[silence:600ms]>

- Sadness slows metabolism, forcing rest (a body’s forced pause button).<[silence:600ms]>

Three related subjects are facial microexpressions, how music hijacks mood, and animal grief rituals.<[silence:600ms]>
clean_story:
Body’s instant signals. Decisions before thinking.

The word emotion comes from Latin emovere—“to move out.” Like a storm shifting sand, emotions push thoughts and actions before logic catches up.

A smile flashes in 0.3 seconds when joy hits, faster than blinking. Fear triggers adrenaline in 1.5 seconds, sharpening focus like a camera zoom. These aren’t just feelings; they’re survival tools. Anger tightens muscles for conflict. Disgust wrinkles the nose to block bad smells. Even “useless” emotions serve: embarrassment’s blush cools overheated skin after stress.

Emotions aren’t chaotic. They follow scripts:
- Surprise lifts eyebrows to widen vision (like a deer freezing at rustling leaves).
- Sadness slows metabolism, forcing rest (a body’s forced pause button).

Brain scans show emotions light up the amygdala—two almond-sized clusters—before the “thinking” cortex even wakes up. That’s why you duck before hearing “ball!” or cry at a movie’s fake death.

Three related subjects are facial microexpressions, how music hijacks mood, and animal grief rituals.
